zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Swanson於1396年12月6日出生在英國素瑞可洛爾斯特時，她的父親Nicholas Swanson20歲，母親Elizabeth Bunne20歲。她於大約1418年與John Gaynsford IV在英國英格蘭素瑞可洛爾斯特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1424年在英國英格蘭素瑞可洛爾斯特享年29歲。

nameMeanings.name: Swanson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nils, Nels, Sven, Lars, Thor, Helmer, Iver, Alf, Astrid, Eskil, Hilmer.
Americanized form of Swedish, Norwegian, Danish, and North German   Svensson  , and Norwegian and Danish   Svensen   or   Svendsen  , cognates of 1.

Scottish (Caithness) and English: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Swan, an Anglicized form of Old Norse Sveinn (from sveinn ‘lad’), + -son. In northern England, Middle English Swanson was in fact an occasional variant of Swainson . In some cases the name may also arise from Middle English swan(e), swon(e) ‘swineherd, peasant, male servant’ (Old English swān) + -son, but this is unlikely to be the usual origin. See Swan .

variants.name: Svensson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Anders, Erik, Lennart, Sven, Ejnar, Johan, Bent, Bjorn, Gunn, Holger, Mats.  German Ernst, Kurt, Markus, Otto.

Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Sven, from Old Norse Sveinn, originally a byname meaning ‘boy, servant’. It is also found in Denmark, northern Germany, and Norway. Compare Svenson , Swainson , Swanson , Swenson , Swensson , and Swinson .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Swanson was born on 6 December 1396, in Crowhurst, Surrey, England, her father, Nicholas Swanson, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth Bunne, was 20. She married John Gaynsford IV about 1418, in Crowhurst, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1424, in Crowhurst, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Swanson est née le 6 décembre 1396, à Crowhurst, Surrey, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Swanson, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bunne, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé John Gaynsford IV environ 1418, à Crowhurst, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1424, à Crowhurst, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans.

Sie starb um 1424 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Crowhurst, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Swanson(이)가 1396년 12월 6일 영국 수리 크로화르스트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Swanson(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Bunne(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1418년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 크로화르스트에서 John Gaynsford IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1424년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 크로화르스트에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.
